title: Rich Vietnamese Bread Bake
description: A rich and cozy Vietnamese dish featuring white bread mix, wood-fired-bake, and FODMAP-friendly ingredients, resulting in a bouncy-qq texture.
instructions: Preheat the wood-fired oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large mixing bowl, combine the white bread mix, eggs, coconut milk, granulated sugar, active dry yeast, and salt.
Mix the ingredients together until a smooth dough forms.
Knead the dough for 10 minutes until it becomes elastic.
Place the dough in a greased bowl, cover it with a damp cloth, and let it rise in a warm place for 1 hour.
Punch down the dough and shape it into a round loaf.
Brush the melted unsalted butter on top of the loaf and sprinkle with sesame seeds.
Bake the loaf in the preheated wood-fired oven for 25 minutes.
Remove the loaf from the oven and let it cool on a wire rack for 10 minutes.
Sprinkle the wood-fired-bake seasoning on top of the loaf before serving.
